_comment: Source-card registry for the semantic-investigation sample. Each card must be LOCALLY MEANINGFUL (doc 17): a claim_scope stating what it backs. official-doc cards carry a URL + claim_scope; curated-note cards also point at a checked-in notes/*.md so resolvability is verifiable offline. URL-only entries without claim_scope are REJECTED by the validator (rule: no invisible sources).

sources.label: Apache Solr Reference Guide
sources.claim_scope: Solr index, schema, query, shard, replica, and SolrCloud coordination concepts.
sources.summary: Official Solr documentation covering the inverted index, managed schema, the query parser/eDisMax, sharding by hash, and the Overseer/ZooKeeper coordination model.

sources.label: Curated note: Solr architectural concepts
sources.claim_scope: Internal-model concepts for Solr (index/schema/query/shard/replica) and their relationships, curated for the Portolan investigation contract.
sources.summary: A checked-in curated note explaining how Solr's concepts fit together, so a cold reader understands the component without fetching external docs.

sources.label: Bigtop Solr package recipe (local corpus)
sources.claim_scope: Solr's packaging/integration surface inside the Bigtop distribution (the bigtop-packages/ Solr recipe and version pinning in the BOM).
sources.summary: Evidence that Bigtop packages Solr as a versioned component and wires it into the smoke-test and deployment surface.

sources.label: Apache Spark documentation
sources.claim_scope: Spark RDD/DataFrame, Catalyst optimizer, DAG scheduler, executor model, and structured streaming concepts.
sources.summary: Official Spark docs covering the resilient distributed dataset abstraction, the DataFrame/Dataset API, the Catalyst query optimizer, the DAG scheduler + executors, and the micro-batch streaming model.

sources.label: Curated note: Spark internal model
sources.claim_scope: Internal-model concepts for Spark (RDD, DataFrame, DAG scheduler, executor, Catalyst) curated for the investigation contract.
sources.summary: A checked-in curated note explaining Spark's conceptual architecture for the admiral.

sources.label: Apache Flink documentation
sources.claim_scope: Flink DataStream API, job graph, checkpointing, state backends, and windowing/processing-time model.
sources.summary: Official Flink docs covering the DataStream API, the JobManager/TaskManager graph, exactly-once checkpoint barriers, state backends, and window operators.

sources.label: Curated note: Flink internal model
sources.claim_scope: Internal-model concepts for Flink (DataStream, job graph, checkpoint, state backend, windowing) curated for the investigation contract.
sources.summary: A checked-in curated note explaining Flink's conceptual architecture for the admiral.

sources.label: Curated note: Spark vs Flink overlap analysis
sources.claim_scope: The semantic overlap and contrast between Spark and Flink across streaming model, API surface, state semantics, and latency model.
sources.summary: A checked-in curated note analysing where Spark and Flink overlap (distributed compute over Hadoop) and where they diverge (micro-batch vs true streaming), supporting the bidirectional overlaps_with/contrasts_with relations.

sources.label: Apache Attic entry for Oozie
sources.claim_scope: Oozie lifecycle (retired, moved to the Attic) and its legacy role as a Hadoop workflow scheduler.
sources.summary: Official Apache Attic record marking Oozie as retired and preserving read-only resources, confirming its metadata-only source boundary.

sources.label: Curated note: Oozie internal model
sources.claim_scope: Internal-model concepts for Oozie (workflow, coordinator, bundle, HCatalog integration) and the not_assessed boundaries forced by its retired/metadata-only state.
sources.summary: A checked-in curated note explaining Oozie's conceptual architecture and explicitly marking which parts cannot be verified from the current corpus.

sources.label: Bigtop BOM / support matrix (local corpus)
sources.claim_scope: Component version declarations and integration relationships inside the Bigtop 3.5.0 distribution.
sources.summary: Evidence of how Bigtop pins component versions and declares dependency/integration relationships across the ecosystem.

sources.label: Apache Hadoop as corpus hub (local corpus)
sources.claim_scope: Hadoop's role as the filesystem/YARN/MR foundation that Solr, Spark, Flink, Hive, and HBase depend on.
sources.summary: Evidence from the corpus that Hadoop is the ecosystem hub other components depend_on, establishing the downstream/upstream placement of the selected components.
